context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Left

episode_text:
day=9; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Watching TV with TV on Left; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=1 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=fast; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is fruit or another cold-served item THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user selected no microwave for a fruit meal, consistent with preserving intended cold serving temperature. IF the meal is a warm savory item in a front-oriented work context THEN 1 min or 3 min depending on the specific meal BECAUSE the user has selected both 1 min and 3 min for warm savory meals while working on a laptop in front, showing meal-specific variation within that context. IF the meal is a savory item in a social context THEN 1 min or 2 min depending on context and meal BECAUSE the user has selected 1 min in a front-social meal and 2 min in a right-social meal, indicating moderate reheating with some context sensitivity rather than a fixed extreme. IF the meal is a warm savory item while watching TV on the left THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user selected a shorter reheat in that left-TV context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected front-occlusion minimization in front-social and laptop-in-front contexts. IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity to the right of the user THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected right-occlusion minimization in right-oriented contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user selected no occlusion constraint rather than minimizing left occlusion in that left-TV context.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves a social partner or valued activity directly in front of the user THEN medium or fast depending on the meal/context BECAUSE the user previously selected medium in front-oriented contexts and also selected fast while working on a laptop in front, indicating a tendency toward quicker motion when attention is front-focused. IF dining context involves a social partner to the right of the user THEN slow or medium depending on the specific right-side context BECAUSE the user has selected slow in a right-social meal after earlier medium selections in right-oriented contexts, so right-side situations support a cautious non-fast preference rather than a single fixed speed. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN fast BECAUSE the user selected fast in that left-TV context, suggesting a preference to reduce interruption duration there.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food acquisition must remain compatible with limited mouth opening in general THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user's physical profile supports narrower bites and this has been the dominant observed choice. IF the meal is a bite-acquisition-challenging savory item such as chicken and broccoli while watching TV on the left THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-acquisition-success option in that context, indicating occasional willingness to trade narrower bites for acquisition reliability.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF the user's physical constraints are relevant to the meal context THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and observed selections consistently favor the mode that does not require leaning forward.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE this parameter only applies to outside-mouth transfer. IF the user's physical constraints are considered for food delivery THEN not applicable BECAUSE the observed preference and physical profile support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected speech in that context. Otherwise, no stable context-dependent variation is established beyond prior LED selections.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF initiating feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected low-effort automatic initiation in that context, indicating that reducing interaction burden can override explicit control there.

IF initiating drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected low-effort automatic initiation in that context, indicating that reducing interaction burden can override explicit control there.

IF initiating mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user can press buttons reliably, while limited mouth opening makes open-mouth interaction challenging. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected low-effort automatic initiation in that context, indicating that reducing interaction burden can override explicit control there.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN speech BECAUSE the user selected speech in that context. Otherwise, no stable context-dependent variation is established beyond prior LED selections.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF completing feeding transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected the reliable perception-based option and this avoids requiring extra physical input.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF completing drinking transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based drinking completion less suitable. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in that context, indicating a preference to minimize manual interaction there.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF completing mouth-wiping transfer in contexts where physical ability constraints matter and no stronger contextual pressure is observed THEN button BECAUSE the user cannot reliably perform head gestures, making perception-based wiping completion less suitable. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic completion in that context, indicating a preference to minimize manual interaction there.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes a social partner or focal activity directly in front of the user THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose retraction in front-oriented contexts, including both front-social and laptop-in-front meals, consistent with wanting to reduce prolonged visual obstruction of the main focus. IF dining context includes a social partner to the right of the user THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected retraction in right-social contexts, indicating that social presence on the right is sufficient to favor reducing obstruction. IF dining context involves a focal activity to the right without a social partner THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to retract while watching TV on the right, suggesting that right-sided visual activities alone do not trigger a preference for added retraction time. IF dining context involves watching TV on the left THEN no BECAUSE the user selected no retraction in that left-TV context as well, reinforcing a pattern that side-oriented TV viewing does not justify extra retraction time.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose reduced dipping for sauce-containing meals in social contexts, consistent with limiting messiness around others. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves a front-focused activity such as working on a laptop THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected less dipping for sauce-containing meals while working on a laptop in front, indicating a stable tendency to reduce messiness when attention is on a valued front activity. IF food does not include a dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not relevant and the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals. IF the meal has sauce already on the food but no separate dipping action is desired, including in a left-TV context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for a sauced savory meal in that context.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV on the left and the user is willing to rely on automation for transfer initiation/completion THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the shortest wait together with broad autocontinue settings in that context, indicating a preference for quick automatic progression there. Otherwise, no broader context-dependent variation is established yet.
